text:
Once upon a time, there was a humble squirrel who loved collecting acorns. Every autumn, she would fill her cheeks as full as possible, storing them away for winter. Every morning, she would race across the forest floor finding perfect nut buried deep in the fallen leaves.

One peculiar day, while she was picking acorns, she heard a strange singing. Looking around, she spotted a singing bird, its melody out of place among the rustle of leaves. The bird stopped suddenly, shaking its feathered head as if trying to clear its throat.

The squirrel approached curiously. "Hello there!" she chirped. "What are you singing about?"

The bird paused, its pitch-shifted voice echoing through the forest. "I'm singing about peace." Each note seemed to resonate differently with each word. "I want to bring hope to all creatures."

The squirrel listened, enchanted.

"You see," the bird continued, "Every nut I find brings hope. Even though it's just for
